frame_analyses.description: First instrument is visible from the right side; needle being oriented toward the Penrose drain and marked slit.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initial setup controlled and deliberate; no wasted motion but opening orientation not especially fast.

frame_analyses.description: Aligning the needle with the first bite near the intended mark on the drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Steady needle handling; exact target accuracy hard to judge from phone-recording-of-monitor format.

frame_analyses.description: Second bite being completed; needle brought through the opposite side of the slit.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Bite placement close to marks; gentle tissue handling, no avulsion or tearing.

frame_analyses.description: Both suture ends drawn out and positioned for the first knot throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean transition from placement to tying; some extra slack that later slows knot construction.

frame_analyses.description: Double wrap formed; first throw appears to be a surgeon's knot with right instrument leading.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Correct double first throw; seats without obvious slip.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw created with opposite hand leading; loop passed and tightened.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate hand switching; throw lays flatter than the first and remains controlled.

frame_analyses.description: Third required throw formed and seated, switching back to original leading hand.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First three required throws technically acceptable and secure; no obvious unraveling.

frame_analyses.description: Additional knot work continues after the required third throw, suggesting at least one extra security throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Extra throws not inherently unsafe but add substantial time and reduce efficiency.

frame_analyses.description: Further tightening and another apparent reinforcing throw before transition to cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Main efficiency loss — knot seems already secure, so extra cycles likely only cost time.

frame_analyses.description: Instrument positioning shifts toward tail trimming near the knot stack.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Slow cut setup with repeated regrasping and less decisive instrument placement than during needle passage.

frame_analyses.description: Repeated cut attempts continue around the knot; one instrument stabilizing, other working near tails.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Endgame efficiency drops markedly; this phase consumes a large fraction of total run.

frame_analyses.description: Video ends with instruments still near the knot; complete cutting of both ends not definitively visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Endpoint not perfectly clear; time and score below are conservative using end of clip as completion proxy.

knot_assessments.notes: Correct double wrap surgeon's knot with right-hand lead; seats without obvious loosening.
Appropriate hand switch; laid as single throw; no obvious crossing error or slip.
Switches back to right hand and seats adequately. At least one additional security throw follows — adds time but does not destabilize the knot.

technique_summary: V17 scored at 160.8s / 436.2 FLS (best proxy — clip ends while cut phase still active so endpoint is provisional). Acceptable needle passage; first three required throws appear correctly constructed with appropriate hand alternation, no obvious slip, no drain avulsion. Major weakness is efficiency after the knot is already secure — additional security throws and prolonged cut sequence add substantial time. The required three-throw knot was likely complete by ~100-108s; remaining ~55s is over-tying + slow cut.
improvement_suggestions: Stop after the required third throw unless the knot is clearly insecure — extra reinforcing throws appear to add ~30s.
Shorten and control the working loop during tying so each throw is built closer to the knot with fewer regrasps.
Move earlier and more decisively into the cut phase — the finishing sequence is the slowest part and complete trimming is not clearly demonstrated before clip ends.
strengths: Needle passage controlled; bites close to intended marks without obvious tissue trauma or drain avulsion.
First three required throws follow the correct pattern: double first throw, then alternating single throws with appropriate hand switching.
Knot security appears acceptable on video, with no obvious unraveling after seating.
comparison_notes: Paired with score_claude_V17_001 — see memory/comparisons/V17_teacher_comparison_20260407.json. ΔFLS 1.2 (strong agreement). Same drain-gap divergence on monitor recording: Claude flags partial closure with small gap, GPT-4o sees near-complete. Unusual phase pattern: Claude puts more time in early throws (37s vs GPT-4o 22s), GPT-4o puts more time in late throws + cut — different from the standard third_throw/cut mirror seen in V11/V12/V14/V16. Both teachers note the completion endpoint is unclear.
